title: Software Engineering BEng (Hons): Programme Overview
text: Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. From mobile banking apps to aircraft autopilot controls, you’ll learn how to analyse software requirements, and then design, build and test a system that meets them. On this degree you’ll combine hands-on software development with a solid knowledge base of software engineering. Specialist modules will let you explore new developments in areas such as: robotics systems cyber security cloud application development Facilities Our computer lab offers facilities that span software projects, computing laboratory and cyber-security.
summary: Software Engineering BEng (Hons) - Programme Overview: Learn to develop reliable, complex and secure software systems. From mobile banking apps to aircraft autopilot controls, you’ll learn how to analyse software requirements, and then design, build and test a system that meets them.

title: Software Engineering BEng (Hons): What You Will Study
text: The programme structure is designed to give you experience of core software technologies and techniques, while making it possible for you to work on your areas of specialised interest in depth. Year 1 overview The first year of the Software Engineering programme introduces you to the fundamental mathematics and theory that underpin the discipline, and gives hands-on experience of programming, computer hardware, computer networks and operating systems. You’ll study core topics such as: programming data management software modelling and design Year 2 overview The second year of the Software Engineering programme consolidates the material from the first year with an integrated software development module that runs throughout the year, and broadens out the curriculum to include functional programming, artificial intelligence, and formal specification and verification You’ll deepen your understanding of software engineering by studying topics, like: intelligent systems software design interaction design A group project will let you work within a team to design, create and test a software system. It will give you practical experience and deep exposure to teamwork and project management. Year 3 overview You’ll carry out an individual project in an area of software engineering that interests you. An academic, who is conducting research in that field, will provide support throughout. You’ll choose from a range of specialist Software Engineering modules, from across computer science and electronics. This will give you a deeper knowledge and let you explore topics such as: robotic systems machine learning cyber security computer vision Programme structure You’ll take core and compulsory modules in the first 2 years. In the third year you’ll complete an individual project and choose from a range of optional modules. The programmes are designed to ensure that students gain the fundamentals of their discipline and then add specialisation and perspective through available optional modules. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. title: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons): Programme Overview
text: Our BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance is a prestigious degree for students aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. It offers flexibility in career planning. Students can work towards the requirements of global firms, professional preparation and certification, or research. Learn how to prepare and interpret financial and managerial information and gain a sound understanding of investment and finance, while shaping the degree towards your objectives. The degree is mapped to the latest developments in financial reporting, from sustainability reporting through to international reporting standards. The School of Business at University of Southampton is the only business school in Europe that holds a licence to impart training in Sustainability Reporting. You will graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years, simultaneously gaining practical experience through client projects and keeping pace with preparations for professional certifications and qualifications. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that the programme will: Take students towards a wide range of professional goals, while conveniently completing a rigorous and globally recognised undergraduate degree. Offer a window into International Financial Reporting Standards that are closely aligned to UK Financial Reporting Standards for a global picture, while being contextualised to India. Provide valuable accreditations that entitle students to earn specific exam exemptions for professional bodies including: Association of Chartered Certified Accountants (ACCA) Association of International Accountants (AIA) Chartered Institute of Management Accountants (CIMA) Chartered Institute of Public Finance and Accountancy (CIPFA) Institute of Chartered Accountants of Scotland (ICAS)
summary: Accounting and Finance BSc (Hons) - Programme Overview: Our BSc (Hons) Accounting and Finance is a prestigious degree for students aiming to enter the fields of commerce, accountancy and finance. It offers flexibility in career planning.

text: Students take a number of core and compulsory modules before selecting optional modules based on their interest. Year 2 overview You’ll gain a greater understanding of business essentials such as financial accounting, company law, portfolio theory, and the principles of audit and taxation. Year 3 overview The research dissertation module in year 3 is key to the development of critical thinking. Every student uniformly acquires a research background in the undergraduate degree, emerging as an independent thinker with an Honours degree in three years. Your optional module choices include auditing, strategic management, and futures and options. Programme structure The programme covers foundational concepts in year 1 before building specialisation and perspective through available optional modules in Year 2 and Year 3. title: Business Management BSc (Hons): Programme Overview
text: Learn to think differently about business and inspire change within organisations and society. Our BSc (Hons) Business Management degree offers an interdisciplinary approach, while developing skills and knowledge to navigate complex business environments. The programme covers foundational management through specialised modules in disciplines of accounting and finance, analytics, marketing, and business management. Our research-focused academics are leaders in their field and keep the programme current. Students graduate with an internationally recognised Honours qualification in 3 years. The content is equivalent to a BSc (Hons) Business Management degree delivered to University of Southampton students in the UK. We expect that after graduating students will: gain an understanding of key business management principles and practices, including accounting, marketing, finance, strategy and entrepreneurship tailor the programme to their interests by choosing optional modules learn to thrive in an ever-changing business environment explore sustainability in modern society and contemporary business.

title: Business Management BSc (Hons): What You Will Study
text: This degree will suit you if you want to drive change, and make both business and society better. Each year is made up of core, compulsory and optional modules and optional modules begin from the second semester itself. Year 1 overview Compulsory modules will explore current management ideas from a variety of perspectives. You’ll study how commerce, industry and technology have developed, within a broader social and historical context. You’ll also learn about core management and financial accounting, and develop practical management skills. Year 2 overview Compulsory modules will develop your understanding of management and organisational theory, and business research. The second year envisages that students select six optional modules, or three out of four modules in a semester. You’ll choose from optional modules that cover topics such as: entrepreneurial management innovation, technology and the environment making successful decisions We’ll also teach you the research skills needed to complete your dissertation in year 3. Year 3 overview In part 3, a year-long dissertation or business project runs along with 6 modules to ensure the rigour of an Honours degree. You’ll also choose optional modules covering areas such as: marketing in the digital age international business information, organisation and accountability from a historical perspective Programme structure Our compulsory modules provide a solid grounding in the theory of business management. They show you how to apply your skills to real-world settings. You’ll also choose optional modules that reflect your personal interests or future career options . The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme. Semester abroad You will have the possibility to spend a semester at University of Southampton UK in Year 2, learning in a vibrant academic and cultural context. The selection of students for a semester abroad will depend on academic performance and a decision by programme lead.
summary: Business Management BSc (Hons) - What You Will Study: This degree will suit you if you want to drive change, and make both business and society better. Each year is made up of core, compulsory and optional modules and optional modules begin from the second semester itself.

title: Business Management BSc (Hons): Eligibility Criteria
text: Students must meet overall academic scores and subject-specific scores to be eligible for our programmes. Students studying under CBSE, CISCE and State Boards require Mathematics up to Class X. Class XII Mathematics is not required. In order to be eligible for the programme, students must have: Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your All-India Senior School Certificate Examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary School Examination (Standard X) Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (CISCE): A minimum of 78% (overall) in your Indian School Certificate examination (Standard XII) with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Indian Certificate of Secondary Education (Standard X) State Board: A minimum of 83% (overall) in your Higher Secondary Certificate (Standard XII) examination with a minimum of 75% in Mathematics in your Secondary Certificate Examination (Standard X) International Baccalaureate: 34 points and above, including 17 points at Higher level Offers will be made on the individual Diploma Course subject(s) and the career-related study qualification. The CP core will not form part of the offer. Where there is a subject pre-requisite(s), applicants will be required to study the subject(s) at Higher Level in the Diploma course subject and/or take a specified unit in the career-related study qualification. Applicants may also be asked to achieve a specific grade in those elements. Please see the University of Southampton International Baccalaureate Career-Related Programme (IBCP) Statement for further information. Applicants are advised to contact the Admissions Office for more information. A Levels: AAB, plus Grade B or above in GCSE Maths English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. title: Computer Science BSc (Hons): What You Will Study
text: A strong programming pathway runs through three years with Python as a first language, C as a second, Java as a third followed by Haskell as a fourth language. Through a mix of theoretical, design and practical subjects, you’ll learn about: basic computer science theory the ethical and legal frameworks which computer scientists and software engineers work within the practicalities of working with computers key techniques and application areas Year 1 overview You’ll study the logical and mathematical theory underpinning computer science. You’ll also gain an understanding of the fundamentals of computer hardware. As an introduction to software engineering, you’ll cover data structures and algorithms. You’ll also look at the principles of programming. Year 2 overview You’ll deepen your understanding of computer science by studying topics, such as artificial intelligence, communication protocols and the TCP/IP layered model. Year 3 overview A compulsory final year project is the capstone assessment for the three-year degree. Regular project work ensures teamwork, project planning, time management, presentation taking learning towards the successful completion of a brief. An individual project is a chance to conduct in-depth research in an area of computer science that interests you, under the supervision of an academic with expertise in your chosen field. Cybersecurity, applications and artificial intelligence pathways span diverse modules and you will get to choose optional modules on areas like: advanced databases machine learning technologies natural language processing security of cyber physical systems social computing techniques Programme structure This programme builds a comprehensive foundation in the discipline through inclusion of various modules as compulsory that are otherwise considered elective. Optional modules are geared to employability: they are attractive to students and well-received by industry. It includes research-led, contemporary compulsory modules on areas like formal specification and verification, the design of programming languages and interpreters and on artificial intelligence. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.

title: Creative Computing BSc (Hons): Programme Overview
text: Build immersive and engaging digital experiences that bring art and technology together in our creative computing degree. The programme develops creative, technical and intellectual strengths drawing on industry-standard technology and philosophical and ethical approaches. You will take the construction of digital projects to the screen and beyond using sculpture and fabrication. The programme will enhance strengths and skills in design and systems thinking, to bring focus on programming skills, user experience design, and building digital projects. You will research the aesthetic, sustainable, and political aspects of computer media formats including networks, social media, digital games and open source and activist initiatives. As a creative computing student, you will benefit from: virtual reality, augmented reality, robot design and programming systems access to fabrication and construction equipment exposure to social and collaborative computing guest speakers and industry events chances to showcase your work optional study trips to museums, galleries, and industry events

Year 3 overview The economics dissertation module in Year 3 is a hands-on research project that develops your ability to work independently in the real work environment. A: The University Grants Commission (UGC) has mandated University of Southampton Delhi to make sure that programmes delivered in India are the same as those delivered at University of Southampton in the UK. Three years is the standard length of an undergraduate Honours degree in England where University of Southampton sets academic standards. This 3 year length reflects a trend towards accelerated learning where students see a return on their tuition fees as quickly as possible, whilst still maintaining high academic standards and the quality of the degree awarded. University of Southampton 3 year undergraduate degrees, and 1 year postgraduate degrees, are accepted widely internationally.

The programme stands out for: Specialised knowledge and mastery in the powerful information landscape, that is highly valued in industry and government. A blend of sophisticated Big Data analytics, computer science and creativity involved in designing powerful visualisations. Large-scale analysis and research techniques working with real data Independent research and a dissertation on a subject of your interest. Networking opportunities across a larger research ecosystem (events, collaboration, exposure to industry-scale problems). Application of data techniques and tools in meeting emerging business needs and ambitious societal problems. title: Data Science MSc: What You Will Study
text: The programme structure comprises 2 semesters of taught modules, culminating in a final semester independent (supervised) research project and written dissertation, demonstrating advanced analytical and research skills. Semester 1 focuses on key topics in foundational data science, machine learning, visualisation, and research methods for the postgraduate level. Semester 2 helps you to build on these foundations. After completing the taught component, you will conduct supervised independent research, yielding a master’s dissertation. The 60-credit major project (summer term) gives you space to develop your interests through an independent project. Course modules The set of first semester modules comprises Foundations of Data Science, Data Visualisation and Machine Learning Technologies to introduce core concepts, methods, techniques and tools. A fourth compulsory module prepares you for your final-semester dissertation project. The second semester modules include Data Mining, Social Media and Network Science, Natural Language Processing and Deep Learning Technologies and consolidate conceptual learning and practice using real-world data. Finally, the third semester research project gives you the chance to demonstrate your ability to design and deliver advanced applications, systems and solutions to a tight deadline through a substantial dissertation. For more information, see our Programme Description Document . Programme structure As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.

title: Data Science MSc: Eligibility Criteria
text: The programme is primarily designed for students with a first degree in computing or a closely related degree covering fundamental notions of programming or data analysis. In order to apply for the programme, you require an upper second-class degree with a mark of 65% or above or international equivalent in computer science or a closely related subject: computer science computer engineering software engineering mathematics/statistics physics You must also have a good 2:1 (65%) score or equivalent in the following: 1 programming module (e.g. MATLAB, Python, Java, C, C++, C#, object-oriented or advanced programming language). 1 advanced maths module, ideally statistics and probability. English language requirement We accept several ways of meeting our English Language requirement. These include: IELTS Test, with scores of 6.5 overall (6.0 in reading, writing, speaking, and listening) other tests listed on our website an approved academic qualification having the required grade If you’ve successfully completed a degree, taught and assessed in English, up to 2 years before the date of first enrolment at the University of Southampton, we may be able to accept this as evidence of your English language ability. Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements. You may be invited for an in-person or online interview. We do not require that applicants appear for admission tests as part of the application process. Our academic admissions criteria Typical academic admissions criteria represent the usual level of academic attainment an applicant is expected to achieve prior to being admitted to that programme. These criteria are not intended to provide a guarantee that all applicants who satisfy the criteria may be offered a place, nor that all applicants to be made an offer will receive an identical offer to one another.

In semester 3 (the final 3 months) you’ll complete an individual research project for your dissertation. The course modules cover: management within a global context strategic management marketing accounting and control responsible leadership decision making operations management Programme structure The programme is built on a number of core and compulsory modules, that are designed to ensure that students gain a comprehensive understanding of the fundamentals of business. The programme includes modules such as: Managing within a global context: offered by the Department of Human Resource Management and Organisational Behaviour, this module explores advanced perspectives on management, leadership, power, culture and diversity. Career management in a digital age: developed by a globally recognised expert in the area of career management. Risk-taking and decision-making: this module emphasises how combining qualitative and quantitative thinking enhances effective decision-making in the face of risk and uncertainty. The Programme Description Document contains an overview of the programme, its aims, information on programme modules and shows how we assess student progress as they learn with us. As a research-led University, we undertake a continuous review of our programmes to ensure quality enhancement and to manage our resources. As a result, this programme may be revised during a student’s period of registration; however, any revision will be balanced against the requirement that the student should receive the educational service expected. Please read our Disclaimer to see why, when, and how changes may be made to a student’s programme.
summary: International Management MSc - What You Will Study: This is a full-time 1 year degree programme. The first 9 months of the programme comprises taught study, divided into 2 semesters.

Refer to our English Language proficiency guide for more information about English language requirements.
